description_long: This is a large, single-edged sword with a pronounced, forward-curving blade that widens toward the tip, reminiscent of a falchion or scimitar. The blade has a dark, weathered metallic finish with a sharp, silvery edge, suggesting it is forged from steel or iron. The handle is slightly curved and features a dark grip, possibly made of wood or wrapped material, ending in a faceted pommel. A distinct crossguard with angular, metallic detailing separates the blade from the handle, providing both protection and visual interest. The sword's overall design is robust and functional, with subtle decorative elements on the guard and pommel. This weapon would be visually striking and intimidating if encountered in an urban environment, such as a themed event or as a collectible.
description: A large, curved, single-edged sword with a dark, weathered blade, ornate crossguard, and faceted pommel. The handle is dark and slightly curved, complementing the robust, functional design.
description_view_0: The left side of the sword shows the full curvature of the blade, the ornate crossguard, and the faceted pommel.
description_view_1: A top-down view reveals the thinness of the blade and the symmetrical alignment of the handle and guard.
description_view_2: The right side displays the blade's curve and edge, with the handle and crossguard details visible from the opposite angle.
description_view_3: A bottom-up view highlights the blade's thickness and the geometric design of the pommel.
attribute_handle_design: curved, dark grip, faceted pommel, slight ergonomic contour, metallic accents
attribute_ornamentation: geometric metallic detailing on guard and pommel
